advantages: Strong long-form language, explanation, and coding assistance when prompts specify audience, format, and context.
Tiered models let teams route simple tasks to faster/cheaper endpoints and hard tasks to more capable ones.
Large context on supported models supports document-heavy workflows in a single request within token limits.
Safety-oriented alignment can improve steerability and policy-aware refusals versus unconstrained open models.
disadvantages: Strengths are probabilistic—fluency can mask factual errors; verification remains mandatory.
Higher-capability tiers cost more latency and tokens; over-provisioning wastes budget on trivial tasks.
Safety tuning may add hedging or false refusals on borderline benign prompts.
Strengths do not cover live data, physical actions, or deterministic bulk processing without tools and code.
